Transponders & ADS-B.clue: This is the most likely cause when ADS-B FAIL appears but the Mode S transponder still replies to ground interrogation.
choices: GPS position source is invalid or not received
Transponder antenna has high SWR on the run
Mode S transponder code has not been set
TCAS processor has failed its self-test
Transponders & ADS-B.explanation: The GPS position source is invalid.

Transponders & ADS-B.clue: This TCAS behavior depends directly on the maximum cruise speed configured during installation.
Transponders & ADS-B.explanation: RA thresholds scale with max speed.

Transponders & ADS-B.clue: This is the first thing to investigate when TCAS loses targets inside 5 nautical miles while the transponder tests normal.
choices: TCAS antenna pattern and installation location

Transponders & ADS-B.clue: This is required of the pilot to operate in ADS-B mandated airspace with ADS-B Out inoperative.
choices: Revert the transponder to Mode A only
Rely on TCAS for automatic separation

Transponders & ADS-B.clue: This ADS-B parameter, required by 14 CFR 91.227, captures position accuracy and must be verified after a GPS swap.
Transponders & ADS-B.explanation: NACp is the position accuracy metric.

Transponders & ADS-B.clue: This is the required response when a TCAS II CLIMB RA is issued at the aircraft's service ceiling.
choices: Follow the RA and exceed service ceiling
Adjust the RA response to level flight
Ignore the RA and maintain altitude
Transponders & ADS-B.explanation: Level off when climb performance is not available.

Transponders & ADS-B.clue: This pair of items should be checked when ADS-B altitude reads 300 ft high but position is correct.
choices: ADC static source and encoding altimeter calibration
Transponders & ADS-B.explanation: Check static source and altitude encoding.

Transponders & ADS-B.clue: This is the primary source TCAS uses to determine bearing and range to intruder aircraft.
